notes: Skill delta is concentrated entirely in the 'polish' eval (1.00 with skill vs 0.71 baseline). morning-brief and verify pass 100% in BOTH configurations on the objective assertions.

The polish baseline failed exactly the two discipline rules the skill's tone-guide encodes: the 6-12s segment-duration rule (baseline produced 4-5s segments) and the no-generic-CTA rule (baseline appended a 'follow me' ask, did not close on a question).

Critical defect NOT caught by the assertion set: the polish baseline fabricated a scoreline ('Spain 2-1 Germany') absent from the source. The skill's facts-only rule prevented this. Add a no-fabrication assertion in iteration 2.

morning-brief assertions are non-discriminating at iteration 1 (10/10 both configs) — they check structure, not editorial quality. The skill's real morning-brief edge (richer brief jsonb, correct verification='unverified', omitting DB-generated id/created_at) shows only in grading notes. Consider promoting these to assertions.

The baseline was hand-briefed in every eval prompt with the 4 time-buckets and the content_ideas concept. Production has no such briefing — the skill IS that briefing — so this benchmark understates the skill's operational value.
Timing: with_skill averages 242s vs 216s baseline (+26s) from reading the reference files. Negligible at a 4-videos/day cadence.

verify is a weak discriminator here: the test claim is clearly true and trivially corroborated. A harder fixture (an unverifiable or partially-false claim) would better exercise the skill's verification discipline.
